note_patch.D4: Davos's four eldest sons died in the wildfire at the Blackwater; the loss drives his revenge-resolve, which restarts his Dragonstone arc. fresh-verify-s167 AMEND: the quote ('she gave my sons to the fire. I will cut the living heart from her breast') attributes the drive to MELISANDRE personally, so the source was repointed battle-of-the-blackwater -> melisandre (melisandre MOTIVATES davos-seaworth). Distinct from the existing davos HATES/OPPOSES/FEARS melisandre (emotion/stance) -- MOTIVATES captures that she drives his actions (the assassination attempt).
note_patch.D15: Privately Wyman reveals his enmity toward the Boltons even as he publicly feigns loyalty to Roose, his Warden. fresh-verify-s167 AMEND: the quote names 'the Bastard of Bolton who put Winterfell to the sword' (Ramsay), so the target was repointed roose-bolton -> ramsay-snow to match the evidence. The hidden opposition that powers the GNC.
note_patch.S19: MARQUEE cross-half seam. Jon cites the king's-blood-for-magic danger as why Aemon -- a Targaryen -- must leave the Wall; that journey kills him. fresh-verify-s167 AMEND (tier-2 upheld): Jon's quote frames the danger GENERICALLY ('if the red woman requires king's blood for her spells') and does NOT name the Edric-Storm leeching; leeching-of-edric-storm is retained as the correct in-graph precedent event for that danger, but the causal link is inferential (the leeching is the precedent, not invoked by name) -- hence tier-2, not tier-1.

bug_repoint.note: fresh-verify-s167: repointed source battle-of-the-blackwater -> melisandre; the evidence quote attributes Davos's revenge-drive to Melisandre personally, not the battle as event. Quote/evidence_ref (asos-davos-02) now correctly support melisandre MOTIVATES davos-seaworth.
fresh-verify-s167: repointed target roose-bolton -> ramsay-snow; the quote names the Bastard of Bolton (Ramsay), not Roose.
